---
title: "Overall strategy"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

The overall strategy for representing a set of graph relationships (edges from one variable to another variable from a particular contenxt) is the following:

- Identify minimum separable index sets.
- Do index inversion separately for each separable index set and compose the results.
- Create index inversion methods for different cases.  In particular, methods will be needed for:

    - Blank or constant indices.
    - Index blocks with simple index shifts.  E.g.: `y[i + 1] <- foo(x[i])`.
    - Arbitrary index mapping.  E.g.: `y[i] <- foo(x[ k[i] ])` where `k` is a constant index vector.
